shlokas.original:
ततो रावणनीतायाः सीतायाः शत्रुकर्शनः।
इयेष पदमन्वेष्टुं चारणाचरिते पथि॥
shlokas.transliteration:
tato raavana-neetaayaah seetaayaah shatru-karshanaH.
iyesha padam anveshTum chaarana-aacharite pathi.
shlokas.translation: Then, the destroyer of enemies (Hanuman) wished to search for traces of Sita, who had been carried away by Ravana, on the path frequented by celestial singers.

shlokas.original:
स्वस्ति प्रजाभ्यः परिपालयन्तां
न्यायेन मार्गेण महीं महीशाः।
गोब्राह्मणेभ्यः शुभमस्तु नित्यं
लोकाः समस्ताः सुखिनो भवन्तु॥
shlokas.transliteration:
svasti prajaabhyaH paripaalayantaam
nyaayena maargena maheem maheeshaaH.
go-braahmaNebhyaH shubham astu nityam
lokaaH samastaaH sukhino bhavantu.
shlokas.translation: May the rulers protect the people with justice. May there always be auspiciousness for cows and brahmins. May all the worlds be happy.

shlokas.original:
यस्य विक्रममासाद्य राक्षसा निधनं गताः।
तमेव चिन्तयन्नास्ते यदा सो ऽभूत् कपिः पथि॥
shlokas.transliteration:
yasya vikramam aasaadya raakshasaa nidhanam gataaH.
tam eva chintayann aaste yadaa so'bhoot kapiH pathi.
shlokas.translation: By whose valor the demons met their destruction — that very Rama did the monkey contemplate as he set upon his path.

shlokas.original:
अथोत्तरां दिशं रम्यां प्रतिष्ठे स्वर्गसन्निभाम्।
जगाम लम्बशिखरं महेन्द्रं पर्वतोत्तमम्॥
shlokas.transliteration:
atho uttaraam disham ramyaam pratishThe svarga-sannibhaam.
jagaama lamba-shikharam mahendram parvatottamam.
shlokas.translation: Then he proceeded towards the delightful northern direction, resembling heaven, and reached the great Mount Mahendra with its tall peaks.

shlokas.original:
मेरुमन्दरसंकाशं तोयदप्रतिमं गिरिम्।
धातुसाहस्रचित्रं तं नानाद्रुमलतावृतम्॥
shlokas.transliteration:
meru-mandara-sankaasham toyada-pratimam girim.
dhaatu-saahasra-chitram tam naanaa-druma-lataa-vritam.
shlokas.translation: That mountain resembled Meru and Mandara, rivaled the rain clouds in stature, was adorned with thousands of mineral ores, and was covered with various trees and creepers.
It was thronged by Siddhas and Charanas, frequented by great serpents, richly painted with mineral ores, and filled with wild beasts and deer.

shlokas.original:
स तं समारुह्य गिरिं महात्मा
प्रकम्पयन् सानुमतां महात्मा।
स लक्ष्मणस्यार्य समागमं तं
ददर्श वीरो विपुलं समुद्रम्॥
shlokas.transliteration:
sa tam samaaruhya girim mahaatmaa
prakampayan saanumataam mahaatmaa.
sa lakshmanasya aarya samaagamam tam
adadarsha veero vipulam samudram.
shlokas.translation: The great-souled one climbed that mountain, shaking its peaks. That hero beheld the vast ocean stretching before him.

shlokas.original:
ततः स नदनदीनाथमशेषं
मकरालयं कपिकुञ्जरः।
चकार गमने मतिं सिन्धोः
सीताशोकविनाशनार्थे॥
shlokas.transliteration:
tataH sa nada-nadee-naatham ashesham
makaraaalayam kapi-kunjaraH.
chakaara gamane matim sindhoH
seetaa-shoka-vinaashana-arthe.
shlokas.translation: Then that elephant among monkeys resolved to cross the ocean — the lord of rivers and streams, the abode of crocodiles — to end Sita's sorrow.

shlokas.original:
आत्मानमभिसंस्तभ्य प्राणानभिनिगृह्य च।
ध्यात्वा मनसि गत्वा च बभूवोत्प्लवने कृतः॥
shlokas.transliteration:
aatmaanam abhisanstabhya praaNaan abhinigrihya cha.
dhyaatvaa manasi gatvaa cha babhoov utplavane kritaH.
shlokas.translation: Steadying himself, restraining his breath, concentrating his mind and focusing his thoughts, he prepared himself for the mighty leap.

shlokas.original:
स चिक्षेप तदा वीरो लाङ्गूलं रणकर्कशः।
प्रगृह्य च ततो लाङ्गूलमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
shlokas.transliteration:
sa chikshepa tadaa veero laanguulam raNa-karkashaH.
pragrihya cha tato laanguulam idam vachanam abraveet.
shlokas.translation: That hero, fierce in battle, swung his tail. Grasping his tail firmly, he then spoke these words.
